text: use the "instruct" option less. Only when main agent comes to a point of completeness and wondering what to do next.

status.comment: Updated watchdog prompts to use 'instruct' flag more conservatively. Now only uses instruct (which prepends full INSTRUCT.md + session memory context) when agent is truly idle and explicitly needs direction. Added clear guidelines: USE instruct only when (1) agent asks 'what should I do next?', (2) clearly completed all work and is idle, (3) stuck/unsure. DO NOT use if actively working, just needs simple nudge, or mid-task. This reduces unnecessary token usage while maintaining autonomous guidance when truly needed. Updated both codex-session and claude-session watchdogs.
